note: Function-level gate decision is binary (cover|exclude). User-facing surface that is DEFERRED (not dropped) is recorded as exclude with a reason beginning 'DEFER (data-gated)' + the precise decoder blocker; each such surface's 1st-party usage-share is measured in P2 (SCOPE ORACLE) and listed in evidence Scope Classification. Pure infra (admin/callback/view/simulation) is exclude-by-category. Decoded: `swap` (0x07ed2379) + `clipperSwap` (0xd2d374e5) + `clipperSwapTo` (0xc4d652af) — the latter two via the address_from_uint256 $fn (P3 follow-up round).

0x07ed2379.name: swap
0x07ed2379.reason: user aggregation swap: swap(executor,SwapDescription,data); dstToken/srcToken/amount/minReturnAmount/dstReceiver all in calldata → AmmVenue::AggregatorRoute (OneInchV6), single_emit amm swap

0x83800a8e.name: unoswap
0x83800a8e.reason: unoswap direct/multi-hop swap; srcToken/amount/minReturn/pool/direction/recipient decoded -> Amm::Swap AggregatorRoute(OneInchV6); token_out omitted (pool token0/token1 read needed) -- the static fields bound fund safety (amount_in/min_amount_out/recipient). unoswap.

0xd2d374e5.name: clipperSwap
0xd2d374e5.reason: Clipper MM swap: dstToken is a plain address, srcToken is a packed AddressLib uint256 unmasked via the address_from_uint256 $fn (low 160 bits) → AmmVenue::AggregatorRoute (OneInchV6), single_emit amm swap. recipient = msg.sender ($tx.from).

0xc4d652af.name: clipperSwapTo
0xc4d652af.reason: Clipper MM swap with explicit recipient: srcToken packed AddressLib uint256 unmasked via address_from_uint256 $fn → single_emit amm swap. recipient = $args.recipient.

0x9fda64bd.name: fillOrder
0x9fda64bd.reason: Taker/resolver-side fill, NOT the wallet maker. The maker-facing LOP surface (the EIP-712 Order signature + cancelOrder/cancelOrders) is now COVERED (P1 follow-up). fillOrder is decodable later as Amm::SettleIntentOrder (the Order tuple is calldata uint256 -> address_from_uint256 $fn); deferred because the typical caller is a resolver/searcher, not the signing user.

0xf497df75.name: fillOrderArgs
0xf497df75.reason: Taker/resolver-side fill with args; same taker-side disposition as fillOrder (maker surface covered in P1; decodable later as SettleIntentOrder).

0xcc713a04.name: fillContractOrder
0xcc713a04.reason: DEFER (data-gated): LOP v4 ERC-1271 contract-order fill (taker-side); same packed-Order requirement.

0x56a75868.name: fillContractOrderArgs
0x56a75868.reason: DEFER (data-gated): LOP v4 contract-order fill with args (taker-side); same packed-Order requirement.

0xb68fb020.name: cancelOrder
0xb68fb020.reason: 1inch LOP v4 maker on-chain cancellation: cancelOrder(makerTraits, orderHash) -> Amm::CancelIntentOrder on the OneInchLimitOrder venue (P1 follow-up). order_hash = the bytes32 orderHash arg; single_emit.

0x89e7c650.name: cancelOrders
0x89e7c650.reason: 1inch LOP v4 maker batch cancellation: cancelOrders(makerTraits[], orderHashes[]) -> array_emit Amm::CancelIntentOrder per bytes32 orderHash (bare $inputs scalar element) on the OneInchLimitOrder venue (P1 follow-up).

0x5816d723.name: permitAndCall
0x5816d723.reason: DEFER (data-gated): permit + self-call wrapper (executes an ERC-2612/Permit2 grant then re-enters the router with inner calldata). NOT a silent drop of a permission primitive — needs multicall-style recursion into the inner selector + embedded permit sub-decode; the value-bearing inner leg (usually swap/unoswap) is what carries policy weight. Usage-share measured in P2.

0x05b1ea03.name: bitsInvalidateForOrder
0x05b1ea03.reason: maker order-nonce bookkeeping (bit-invalidator) — low-level order lifecycle, not a value/permission decision a user reasons about pre-sign

0x0d2c7c16.name: advanceEpoch
0x0d2c7c16.reason: maker order-epoch bookkeeping (series invalidation) — low-level order lifecycle, not a pre-sign value/permission surface

0xc3cf8043.name: increaseEpoch
0xc3cf8043.reason: maker order-epoch bookkeeping — low-level order lifecycle, not a pre-sign value/permission surface

0xe413f48d.name: curveSwapCallback
0xe413f48d.reason: swap callback invoked by a Curve pool mid-swap — never user-initiated; reverts unless called by the router's own swap flow

0xfa461e33.name: uniswapV3SwapCallback
0xfa461e33.reason: Uniswap V3 swap callback invoked by a pool mid-swap — never user-initiated

0x8456cb59.name: pause
0x8456cb59.reason: admin (owner-only) emergency pause — governance, not user pre-sign

0x3f4ba83a.name: unpause
0x3f4ba83a.reason: admin (owner-only) unpause — governance, not user pre-sign

0x78e3214f.name: rescueFunds
0x78e3214f.reason: admin (owner-only) fund rescue — governance, not user pre-sign

0xf2fde38b.name: transferOwnership
0xf2fde38b.reason: admin (owner-only) ownership transfer — governance, not user pre-sign

0x715018a6.name: renounceOwnership
0x715018a6.reason: admin (owner-only) ownership renounce — governance, not user pre-sign

0xbd61951d.name: simulate
0xbd61951d.reason: staticcall simulation helper (reverts with the encoded result) — not a real user transaction

Order.reason: EIP-712 LOP v4 `Order` the maker signs off-chain -> typed-data Amm::SignIntentOrder on the OneInchLimitOrder venue (P1 follow-up). domain '1inch Aggregation Router' v6, verifyingContract = the v6 router, primaryType Order; the EIP-712 Order declares maker/asset fields as `address` (resolve directly), valid_until via maker_traits_expiry $fn, recipient via coalesce_address $fn.
